articles.title: Euro rises against Canadian Dollar due to lower oil prices, risk-on mood
articles.expand: EUR/CAD extends its winning streak for the fourth consecutive day, trading around 1.6080 during the European hours on Monday.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: Seen above 0.60 against US Dollar in 2H26 – ING
articles.expand: ING’s FX Strategist Francesco Pesole expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand to deliver a hawkish hold at the 27 May meeting, while warning markets underprice the risk of a surprise hike. He sees new projections signalling tightening from 3Q and looks for two 25bp hikes starting in July.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bullish setup holds, backing case for further gains
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair reverses an intraday dip to sub-1.3800 levels and fills a modest weekly bearish gap, hitting a fresh daily top during the first half of the European session on Monday.

articles.expand: To implement support to curb household utility, gas bills from July to September Will allocate ¥500 billion from reserves for related subsidies Will compile extra budget of more than ¥3 trillion To create new budget reserves to address impact of higher energy costs caused by Middle East conflict Will issue new deficit financing bonds to finance ext...

articles.title: Swiss Franc extends its gains as the US Dollar faints amid Iran peace hopes
articles.expand: The Swiss Franc (CHF) accelerates its recovery against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday, with the USD/CHF pair reaching 11-day lows below 0.7820 at the moment of trading, down from highs past 0.7900 last week.

articles.title: Euro: Downside risks against US Dollar towards 1.1400 support – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the European Central Bank's (ECB) April Account to underline a tightening bias, with markets already pricing high odds of a June rate hike to 2.25%.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Intervention needs BoJ support – HSBC
articles.expand: HSBC analysts argue that foreign exchange intervention alone is unlikely to keep the USD/JPY pair sustainably below 160. They stress that effectiveness increases when intervention coincides with Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hikes and lower Oil prices.

articles.title: Australian Dollar rises as improving sentiment outweighs decreasing RBA rate hike odds
articles.expand: AUD/USD holds ground after two days of losses, trading around 0.7170 during the early European hours on Monday. The pair appreciates as the Australian Dollar (AUD) receives support from fading safe-haven demand in anticipation of the United States (US)-Iran accord.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: Rangebound versus US Dollar on RBNZ repricing – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the RBNZ to hold the Official Cash Rate (OCR) at 2.25% this week, while swaps price 125 bps of tightening over the next year.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Weakens against Dollar on inflation focus – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Teppei Ino reviews recent USD/JPY performance, noting that the pair opened at 156.86 and rose toward 158.50 as the Dollar strengthened on difficult US-Iran negotiations, higher crude oil prices and renewed inflation concerns.

articles.title: US President Trump swears in Kevin Warsh as new Federal Reserve chair
articles.expand: Bond investors expect the new Federal Reserve chair to prioritise inflation control over political pressure to lower interest rates.

articles.title: Russia’s economy is much worse than it seems, and ‘elites are increasingly alarmed’ as alternate GDP gauge shows huge contraction
articles.expand: While Russia has claimed its GDP expanded by about 13% between 2020 and 2024, Sweden's analysis of nighttime luminosity shows the economy actually shrank by 8% during that span.

articles.title: Britain's Youth Employment Crisis and the Rising Cost of Hiring

articles.expand: US-Iran peace talks whipsawed every major market this week, while a hawkish Fed, a new Fed Chair, and cratering consumer sentiment made sure no one stayed comfortable.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.

articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) stalled its recovery from six-week lows near 1.3300 against the US Dollar (USD), as sellers continued to lurk above the 1.3450 region.
